title: Remotes, Tracking, Fetch, Pull, and Push · Recovery Case: Fetch Observation
summary: Synchronize with a shared repository while separating network transfer, local integration, authentication, and authorization decisions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git fetch observation increment in a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Fetch explicit remotes and refspecs, inspect updates, and compare local work before integrating.
Distinguish remote names, URLs, remote-tracking references, local branches, upstreams, and push destinations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Fetch Observation
steps.instruction:
theory case — Local main is 4 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Fetch Observation contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for fetch explicit remotes and refspecs, inspect updates, and compare local work before integrating.

content.text: Local main is 4 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Fetch Observation condition decided your prediction.

content.value: gitworkflows Manual — Controls integration, topic branch, merge, release, and distributed maintainer workflow guidance.

steps.title: Bound Fetch Observation
steps.instruction:
theory case — Local main is 5 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Fetch Observation application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for fetch explicit remotes and refspecs, inspect updates, and compare local work before integrating.

content.text: Fetch explicit remotes and refspecs, inspect updates, and compare local work before integrating. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Local main is 4 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push. A defensible solution applies Fetch Observation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook.

content.code:
git remote -v
git fetch --prune origin
git log --left-right --graph --oneline main...origin/main
git push --dry-run origin main:main
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: git fetch modifies the current branch and working files. This fails the Fetch Observation contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Fetch Observation evidence

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Fetch Observation into observable performance evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Fetch explicit remotes and refspecs, inspect updates, and compare local work before integrating.
